description:
Public prayer (mi sheberakh) for a representative of the merchants ("neʾeman ha-soḥarim") named Moshe ha-Kohen in gratitude for his works of charity on behalf of the poor, scholars, synagogues, and yeshivas. The prayer is entirely in Hebrew and mostly formulaic until the final line where a custom wish in Judaeo-Arabic was added after "may God grant all his wishes": that Moshe be reunited with Abū Naṣr his sister's son (mentioned also in T-S Ar.42.213 (PGPID 21145)). (Information in part from Goitein's index card.)

See T-S 8J7.9 (PGPID 2200) for a list of related documents.
description_he: תפילת מי שברך לנציג הסוחרים משה הכהן על מעשי צדקתו; בעברית; עם בקשה ביהודית-ערבית שיתאחד עם אחיינו
